name: Capital Area Taiwanese
description_short: Capital Area Taiwanese Federal Credit Union is a small, member-owned credit union chartered in 1977 serving 524 members with $12.7M in assets.
description_long:
Capital Area Taiwanese Federal Credit Union was chartered in 1977 and is headquartered in Gainesville, Virginia. As a federally chartered credit union regulated by the NCUA, it operates as an associational-fraternal credit union serving a specific membership base. The credit union maintains a "well-capitalized" status with a net worth ratio of 11.66, significantly above the 7.0 threshold required for that designation.

The credit union offers traditional banking services including share accounts (deposits), loans, and basic banking services to its 524 members. As of Q1 2024, the institution held $12.7M in total assets, with $11.2M in member shares and deposits and $8.6M in total loans outstanding. The institution provides auto loans and mortgages based on available loan trend reporting capabilities, though specific product details are limited in accessible documentation.

Capital Area Taiwanese FCU distinguishes itself as a very small, specialized credit union with membership based on associational-fraternal affiliation rather than occupational or geographic membership. The institution operates with minimal overhead—just 1 full-time and 1 part-time employee across a single branch location with no ATM network. Leadership includes CEO Ming-shyong Wu, and the credit union maintains direct member contact at (301) 916-1008.

The credit union faced operational challenges in Q1 2024, reporting a net loss of $8,025 despite positive net interest income of $13,398. Non-interest expenses of $21,433 exceeded non-interest income of just $10, creating a negative bottom line. While the institution remains well-capitalized, the small membership base, minimal staffing, and recent operating loss suggest significant constraints on service capacity and sustainability. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificate of deposit (CD) or investment products
pros: Well-capitalized status with net worth ratio of 11.66, well above the 7.0 minimum threshold
NCUA-regulated federal credit union with member share insurance protection
Long operational history since 1977 demonstrates institutional continuity
Associational-fraternal membership model serves a defined community
Offers multiple loan products including auto loans and mortgages
Direct member access via phone line (301) 916-1008
cons: Reported Q1 2024 net loss of ($8,025), indicating operational challenges and sustainability concerns
Single branch location with no ATM network severely limits accessibility
Only 1 full-time and 1 part-time employee restricts service capacity and availability
Very small membership base of 524 members limits service scope and institutional resources
Limited publicly available information about specific product offerings, rates, and fees
best_for: Members of the specific associational-fraternal group eligible for membership
Individuals seeking NCUA-insured, community-based banking with personal relationships
Borrowers already established with the credit union seeking loans or refinancing
diagnosis: Capital Area Taiwanese FCU is best for existing members of its associational-fraternal community who value a small, locally-managed credit union with strong capital ratios. The primary caveat is the institution's extremely limited service capacity (1 FT/1 PT employee, single branch, no ATMs) and recent operating losses, which raise questions about long-term viability and ability to serve members effectively.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
